commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9030cb0d7ef292f354a4080e7a9733919a9cd9d7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a one-time startup detector that force-disables the shared Keychain gate for absolute unbundled executables named `CodexBar`, preserves all `.app` launches, and adds focused tests and development documentation.

Reproducibility: yes. Exact-head runtime logs show the raw SwiftPM executable being detected and disabled, and source inspection establishes the shared Keychain path; a live modal prompt was appropriately not triggered under repository policy.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer must explicitly accept or reject the broad raw-executable credential contract; the changelog cleanup is mechanical but does not resolve that policy decision.

target.title: Avoid repeated CodexBarCache keychain prompts in dev builds
target.body:
## Summary
- Disable Keychain access when CodexBar is launched as an unbundled executable, including SwiftPM's default `.build` output and custom `--scratch-path` builds.
- Preserve normal Keychain behavior for every packaged `CodexBar.app`, regardless of signing mode.
- Keep the process-level disable authoritative if settings later attempt to re-enable Keychain access.

## Why
Raw local executables can have unstable code identities. Existing CodexBar cache and browser-cookie Keychain ACLs can then trigger repeated modal password prompts after rebuilds. An unbundled CodexBar process cannot safely use those credentials, so it now skips Keychain-backed paths for that process.

The original patch disabled all ad-hoc-signed bundles. That was too broad because CodexBar's documented local packaging workflow can produce an ad-hoc-signed `.app`. This rewrite uses the bundle boundary instead:

- `/path/to/CodexBar` -> Keychain disabled
- `/path/to/CodexBar.app/Contents/MacOS/CodexBar` -> unchanged

## Proof
- `swift test --filter 'KeychainPromptCoordinatorTests|ClaudeOAuthKeychainAccessGateTests'`
  - 11 tests passed
  - covers default SwiftPM paths, custom scratch paths, packaged apps, and process-level override behavior
- `make check`
  - SwiftFormat clean
  - SwiftLint: 0 violations
- Structured autoreview: clean after addressing custom scratch-path detection and preserving the public diagnostic getter.

No live Keychain probe was run; validation uses pure path detection and test-only Keychain gates to avoid macOS credential prompts.
target.author: yuxin-qiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: Yuxin-Qiao
